Class StaffFeedbackValidatorTest

java.lang.Object
testing.StaffFeedbackValidatorTest
public class StaffFeedbackValidatorTest extends Object

Class: StaffFeedbackValidatorTest

Description: Unit tests for the StaffFeedbackValidator class. Tests cover all positive and negative cases for validateEmailMessage() and containsInappropriateContent() to ensure the Staff Review and Feedback Hub validation logic behaves correctly under all expected conditions.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description

    Method: setUp()
    void
    testContainsInappropriateContent_CleanText()
    Clean text should not be flagged.
    void
    testContainsInappropriateContent_EmptyString()
    Empty string should not be flagged.
    void
    testContainsInappropriateContent_InappropriateWord()
    Text containing an inappropriate word should be flagged.

    Null input should not be flagged.

    Null input should return a validation error.

    A valid message should pass validation and return an empty string.

  • Field Details

    • validator

      private entityClasses.StaffFeedbackValidator validator

  • Constructor Details

    • StaffFeedbackValidatorTest

      public StaffFeedbackValidatorTest()

  • Method Details

    • setUp

      @BeforeEach public void setUp()

      Method: setUp()

      Description: Initializes a fresh StaffFeedbackValidator before each test to ensure test isolation.

    • testValidateEmailMessage_Null

      @Test public void testValidateEmailMessage_Null()
      Null input should return a validation error. A null message must never be passed to the email system.

    • testValidateEmailMessage_Empty

      @Test public void testValidateEmailMessage_Empty()
      Empty string input should return a validation error. An empty message provides no feedback value.

    • testValidateEmailMessage_WhitespaceOnly

      @Test public void testValidateEmailMessage_WhitespaceOnly()
      Whitespace-only input should return a validation error. A message with only spaces or tabs is not meaningful feedback.

    • testValidateEmailMessage_Valid

      @Test public void testValidateEmailMessage_Valid()
      A valid message should pass validation and return an empty string. This is the happy path — a properly formed message should be allowed through.

    • testContainsInappropriateContent_CleanText

      @Test public void testContainsInappropriateContent_CleanText()
      Clean text should not be flagged. Normal discussion content should pass through without issue.

    • testContainsInappropriateContent_InappropriateWord

      @Test public void testContainsInappropriateContent_InappropriateWord()
      Text containing an inappropriate word should be flagged. Uses an actual word from INAPPROPRIATE_WORDS to ensure the check fires correctly.

    • testContainsInappropriateContent_EmptyString

      @Test public void testContainsInappropriateContent_EmptyString()
      Empty string should not be flagged. There is no content to evaluate so it should return false.

    • testContainsInappropriateContent_Null

      @Test public void testContainsInappropriateContent_Null()
      Null input should not be flagged. The method must handle null gracefully without throwing an exception.

    • testContainsInappropriateContent_MixedCase

      @Test public void testContainsInappropriateContent_MixedCase()
      Mixed case inappropriate word should still be flagged. The check must be case-insensitive to catch all capitalization variants.

    • testContainsInappropriateContent_SubstringNotFlagged

      @Test public void testContainsInappropriateContent_SubstringNotFlagged()
      A word that merely contains an inappropriate substring should NOT be flagged. Whole-word matching via \b must prevent false positives on partial matches.
